      = (\beta^p_{a,p})^{\#} \,;\, (\eta_{p} \rhd a)^{\#}
        \,;\, (\beta^p_{p^{\#},a})^{\#}.
  \]
  Between the two un-sheafified middle terms sits the sheafification of the
  presheaf associator
  \[
    \alpha^p_{a,b,c} :
    (a \otimes_p b) \otimes_p c
    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
    a \otimes_p (b \otimes_p c)
  \]
  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), an isomorphism because
  sheafification is a functor and \(\alpha^p\) is one. The associator is therefore
  the \textbf{five-segment} composite
  \[
    \alpha_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{B},\mathcal{C}}
      = \underbrace{\bigl((\eta_{a \otimes_p b} \rhd c)^{\#}\bigr)^{-1}}_{\text{seg }1}
        \,;\, \underbrace{(\alpha^p_{a,b,c})^{\#}}_{\text{seg }2}
        \,;\, \underbrace{(\beta^p_{a,p})^{\#}}_{\text{seg }3}
        \,;\, \underbrace{(\eta_{p} \rhd a)^{\#}}_{\text{seg }4}
        \,;\, \underbrace{(\beta^p_{p^{\#},a})^{\#}}_{\text{seg }5},
    \quad p = b \otimes_p c,
  \]
  where segments 3--5 jointly realise the left-whiskered unit
  \((a \lhd \eta_{p})^{\#}\) via braiding conjugation,
  an isomorphism as a composite of isomorphisms. Naturality in
  \(\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{B}, \mathcal{C}\) follows from naturality of the unit,
  of the whiskerings, and of the presheaf associator.
\end{proof}

\subsection{Symmetric monoidal coherence by localization transfer}
\label{sec:sgr_localized_monoidal}

The associator (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}), braiding (\cref{def:tensorBraiding})
and unitors (\cref{def:tensorObjUnitIso}, \cref{def:tensorObjRightUnitor}) above are
the structural isomorphisms of a genuine \emph{symmetric monoidal} structure on
\(X.\Modules\). Rather than verify Mac\,Lane's coherence axioms --- pentagon,
triangle, hexagon, and \(\beta_{\mathbf{1},\mathbf{1}} = \mathrm{id}\) --- by hand
for these hand-built isomorphisms, we obtain them all at one stroke from the
following observation: \(X.\Modules\) is the \emph{localization} of
\(\PshMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) at the class \(W\) of morphisms inverted by
sheafification, and a localization of a symmetric monoidal category at a
monoidal class of morphisms is again symmetric monoidal, with every coherence law
proved once and for all. The next blocks record the Mathlib machinery that supplies
this, the single new precondition the project must discharge, and the bridge
identifying the hand-built isomorphisms with the transferred ones.

\begin{theorem}[Monoidal structure on a monoidal localization]
  \label{thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.LocalizedMonoidal}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic},
  \texttt{.Braided}, \texttt{.Functor}).}
  Let \(\mathcal{D}\) be a monoidal category, \(W\) a \emph{monoidal} class of
  morphisms (\(W\) is
  multiplicative and stable under whiskering by an arbitrary object on the left
  and on the right), \(L : \mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{E}\) a localization of
  \(\mathcal{D}\) at \(W\), and \(\varepsilon : L(\mathbf{1}_{\mathcal{D}}) \cong u\)
  a choice of unit comparison. Then the type synonym
  \(\mathrm{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) of \(\mathcal{E}\) carries a
  monoidal category structure --- tensor product, associator
  \(\alpha^{\mathrm{loc}}\), left and right unitors
  \(\lambda^{\mathrm{loc}}, \rho^{\mathrm{loc}}\) --- satisfying the pentagon and
  triangle axioms, and \(L\) becomes a strong monoidal functor onto it,
  with comparison isomorphism
  \(\mu_{X,Y} : L(X) \otimes L(Y) \xrightarrow{\sim} L(X \otimes Y)\) and
  componentwise formulas for the associator, the unitors, and the naturality of
  \(\mu\). If \(\mathcal{D}\) is braided (resp. symmetric), the
  synonym is braided (resp. symmetric) with braiding \(\beta^{\mathrm{loc}}\),
  satisfying the hexagon identities. Crucially the
  structure is carried by the type synonym, \emph{not} by \(\mathcal{E}\) itself,
  so it never collides with any pre-existing structure on \(\mathcal{E}\).
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Pentagon axiom]
  \label{lem:monoidal_pentagon_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.MonoidalCategory.pentagon}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Category}).}
  In any monoidal category the associator satisfies the pentagon identity: for
  objects \(W, X, Y, Z\) the two ways of reassociating
  \(((W \otimes X) \otimes Y) \otimes Z\) into
  \(W \otimes (X \otimes (Y \otimes Z))\) agree,
  \[
    (\alpha_{W,X,Y} \rhd Z) \,;\, \alpha_{W, X \otimes Y, Z}
      \,;\, (W \lhd \alpha_{X,Y,Z})
      = \alpha_{W \otimes X, Y, Z} \,;\, \alpha_{W, X, Y \otimes Z}.
  \]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Triangle axiom]
  \label{lem:monoidal_triangle_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.MonoidalCategory.triangle}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Category}).}
  In any monoidal category, for objects \(X, Y\) the associator and the unitors
  satisfy
  \[
    \alpha_{X, \mathbf{1}, Y} \,;\, (X \lhd \lambda_Y) = \rho_X \rhd Y,
  \]
  relating the right unitor on the left factor to the left unitor on the right
  factor through the associator.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Hexagon axiom]
  \label{lem:braided_hexagon_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.BraidedCategory.hexagon_forward}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Braided.Basic}).}
  In any braided monoidal category the braiding and the associator satisfy the
  hexagon identity: for objects \(X, Y, Z\),
  \[
    \alpha_{X,Y,Z} \,;\, \beta_{X, Y \otimes Z} \,;\, \alpha_{Y,Z,X}
      = (\beta_{X,Y} \rhd Z) \,;\, \alpha_{Y,X,Z}
        \,;\, (Y \lhd \beta_{X,Z}).
  \]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Braiding of the unit on the left]
  \label{lem:braiding_tensorUnit_left_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.braiding_tensorUnit_left}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Braided.Basic}).}
  In a braided monoidal category, for every object \(X\),
  \(\beta_{\mathbf{1}, X} = \lambda_X \,;\, \rho_X^{-1}\): the braiding of the unit
  on the left is the composite of the left unitor with the inverse right unitor.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Unitors agree on the unit]
  \label{lem:unitors_equal_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.MonoidalCategory.unitors_equal}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.CoherenceLemmas}).}
  In any monoidal category the two unitors of the unit object coincide,
  \(\lambda_{\mathbf{1}} = \rho_{\mathbf{1}}\). Together with
  \cref{lem:braiding_tensorUnit_left_mathlib} (at \(X = \mathbf{1}\)) this gives
  \(\beta_{\mathbf{1},\mathbf{1}} = \mathrm{id}\), the symmetry of the unit with
  itself used in the right-unit and commutativity reductions.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Sheafification is a localization at the sheafification class]
  \label{lem:sheafification_isLocalization_mathlib}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Localization}).}
  Module sheafification \((-)^{\#} : \PshMod(R_0) \to \ShMod(R)\) is a
  localization functor for the class \(W\) of morphisms of presheaves of modules
  whose underlying abelian-presheaf morphism is a local isomorphism for the
  Grothendieck topology: Mathlib identifies \(W\) with the inverse image of the
  isomorphism class under sheafification, so sheafification is a localization at
  \(W\). For \(X.\Modules\) this specialises (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification},
  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff}) to \(L = \) module sheafification with
  the same class \(W\). This is the localization hypothesis of
  \cref{thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}.
\end{lemma}

The one new precondition \cref{thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib} requires is that the
sheafification class \(W\) be monoidal. Its substance is already in hand: stability
under right whiskering is the proved \cref{lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso}.

\begin{definition}\leanok
[The sheafification class is monoidal]
  \label{def:W_isMonoidal}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.W_isMonoidal}
  \uses{lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso, def:tensorBraiding,
    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib, lem:sheafification_isLocalization_mathlib}
  The class \(W\) of morphisms of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules inverted
  by sheafification is \emph{monoidal}: it is multiplicative and stable under
  whiskering by an arbitrary presheaf of modules on either side.
  Multiplicativity (containing identities, closed under composition) is inherited
  from the local-isomorphism class through the inverse-image construction. Stability under right
  whiskering --- if \(f \in W\) then \(f \rhd \mathcal{P} \in W\) --- is exactly
  \cref{lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso}. Stability under left
  whiskering follows by conjugating with the symmetric braiding of the presheaf
  monoidal structure (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}): since
  \(\mathcal{P} \lhd f\) equals \(\beta \,;\, (f \rhd \mathcal{P}) \,;\, \beta\) up
  to the braiding isomorphisms (which lie in \(W\)), right-whiskering stability
  transports to the left. By the definition of a monoidal class of morphisms it suffices
  to verify multiplicativity together with these two whiskering stabilities, which
  the above supply.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}\leanok
[Unit comparison for the localized monoidal structure]
  \label{def:localizedMonoidalUnitIso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.localizedMonoidalUnitIso}
  \uses{def:schemeModuleSheafification, def:unitModule, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib,
    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
  The unit comparison isomorphism
  \[
    \varepsilon :
      (\mathbf{1}_{\PshMod})^{\#}
      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathbf{1}_X
  \]
  identifying the sheafification (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) of the
  presheaf monoidal unit --- the structure presheaf viewed as a module over itself
  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) --- with the unit module
  \(\mathbf{1}_X\) of \(X.\Modules\) (\cref{def:unitModule},
  \cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}). It is the datum \(\varepsilon\) required to
  instantiate \cref{thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}\leanok
[The localized symmetric monoidal structure on the module sheaves]
  \label{def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.modulesLocalizedMonoidal}
  \uses{thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib, lem:sheafification_isLocalization_mathlib,
    def:W_isMonoidal, def:localizedMonoidalUnitIso,
    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib, lem:monoidal_pentagon_mathlib,
    lem:monoidal_triangle_mathlib, lem:braided_hexagon_mathlib,
    lem:braiding_tensorUnit_left_mathlib, lem:unitors_equal_mathlib}
  Instantiating \cref{thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib} with
  \(\mathcal{D} = \PshMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (symmetric monoidal,
  \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), \(L = \) module sheafification,
  \(W\) the sheafification class (monoidal by \cref{def:W_isMonoidal}, a
  localization by \cref{lem:sheafification_isLocalization_mathlib}), and
  \(\varepsilon\) the unit comparison \cref{def:localizedMonoidalUnitIso}, yields
  the type synonym \(\mathrm{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\), canonically
  identified with \(X.\Modules\) as a category, and carrying a \emph{symmetric monoidal}
  category structure: associator \(\alpha^{\mathrm{loc}}\), unitors
  \(\lambda^{\mathrm{loc}}, \rho^{\mathrm{loc}}\), braiding \(\beta^{\mathrm{loc}}\),
  with the pentagon, triangle and hexagon identities holding by
  \cref{lem:monoidal_pentagon_mathlib}, \cref{lem:monoidal_triangle_mathlib},
  \cref{lem:braided_hexagon_mathlib} and \(\beta^{\mathrm{loc}}_{\mathbf{1},
  \mathbf{1}} = \mathrm{id}\) by
  \cref{lem:braiding_tensorUnit_left_mathlib}, \cref{lem:unitors_equal_mathlib}.
  The structure lives on the type synonym, deliberately not on \(X.\Modules\)
  itself, so it introduces no coherence clash with the rest of the development.
\end{definition}

\providecommand{\lotimes}{\mathbin{\otimes_{\mathrm{loc}}}}

The bridge between \cref{def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal} and the hand-built
isomorphisms of \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}, \cref{def:tensorBraiding},
\cref{def:tensorObjUnitIso}, \cref{def:tensorObjRightUnitor} must respect a
\emph{type distinction}. The synonym
\(\mathrm{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) shares the underlying
\emph{category} of \(X.\Modules\), but its tensor product
\[
  \mathcal{F} \lotimes \mathcal{G}
    \;=\; \bigl((\mathrm{tensorBifunctor}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon)(\mathcal{F})\bigr)(\mathcal{G})
\]
is \emph{not} the hand-built tensor product
\(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G} =
(\mathcal{F}^{\flat} \otimes_p \mathcal{G}^{\flat})^{\#}\)
(\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}); here \(\mathcal{F}^{\flat}\) denotes the underlying
presheaf of modules of a sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) and \((-)^{\#} = L\) the module
sheafification (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}). The two tensor products
carry the same objects to \emph{different} objects of the category, so the
hand-built associator and the localized associator have different sources and
targets: a bare equality \(\alpha = \alpha^{\mathrm{loc}}\) between them is not a
well-formed statement. The comparison must therefore be threaded through the
object-identification isomorphism \cref{def:tensorObjLocalizedIso} on every tensor
slot.

We record first the Mathlib comparison datum, then the object identification, and
then the bridge equalities themselves.

\begin{lemma}[Strong-monoidality comparison of a monoidal localization]
  \label{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.μ}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic},
  \texttt{.Braided}).}
  In the setting of \cref{thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}, write
  \(L' : \mathcal{D} \to \mathrm{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) for the
  localization functor onto the synonym (equal to \(L\) on objects and
  morphisms). For objects \(P, Q\) of \(\mathcal{D}\) there is a comparison
  isomorphism
  \[
    \mu_{P,Q} : L'(P) \lotimes L'(Q)
      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; L'(P \otimes_p Q),
  \]
  natural in \(P\) and in \(Q\): for \(f : P_1 \to P_2\) and \(g : Q_1 \to Q_2\)
  \[
    \bigl(L'(f) \rhd L'(Q)\bigr) \,;\, \mu_{P_2,Q}
      = \mu_{P_1,Q} \,;\, L'(f \rhd Q),
    \qquad
    \bigl(L'(P) \lhd L'(g)\bigr) \,;\, \mu_{P,Q_2}
      = \mu_{P,Q_1} \,;\, L'(P \lhd g)
  \]
  (\texttt{μ\_natural\_left}, \texttt{μ\_natural\_right}). Through \(\mu\), the
  unit comparison \(\varepsilon' = \varepsilon\), and \(L'\) applied to the
  structure maps of \(\mathcal{D}\), the structure isomorphisms of the synonym
  obey the componentwise formulas
  \[
    (\alpha^{\mathrm{loc}}_{L'P_1, L'P_2, L'P_3})^{\phantom{1}}
      = (\mu_{P_1,P_2} \rhd \cdot)\,;\, \mu_{P_1 \otimes P_2, P_3}
        \,;\, L'(\alpha^p_{P_1,P_2,P_3})
        \,;\, \mu_{P_1, P_2 \otimes P_3}^{-1}
        \,;\, (\cdot \lhd \mu_{P_2,P_3}^{-1}),
  \]
  \[
    \lambda^{\mathrm{loc}}_{L'Q}
      = (\varepsilon'^{-1} \rhd L'Q)\,;\, \mu_{\mathbf{1},Q}
        \,;\, L'(\lambda^p_Q),
    \qquad
    \rho^{\mathrm{loc}}_{L'P}
      = (L'P \lhd \varepsilon'^{-1})\,;\, \mu_{P,\mathbf{1}}
        \,;\, L'(\rho^p_P),
  \]
  \[
    \beta^{\mathrm{loc}}_{L'P, L'Q}
      = \mu_{P,Q} \,;\, L'(\beta^p_{P,Q}) \,;\, \mu_{Q,P}^{-1}
  \]
  (\texttt{associator\_hom\_app}, \texttt{leftUnitor\_hom\_app},
  \texttt{rightUnitor\_hom\_app}, \texttt{braidingNatIso\_hom\_app}; in the last,
  \(\mu\) is the lax-monoidal structure map of \(L'\) and \(\mu^{-1}\) its
  oplax counterpart).
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}

\leanok
[Object identification: hand-built versus localized tensor]
  \label{def:tensorObjLocalizedIso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjLocalizedIso}
  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal,
    def:schemeModuleSheafification, lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib,
    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
  For sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) the
  \emph{object-identification isomorphism}
  \[
    i_{\mathcal{F},\mathcal{G}} :
      \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}
      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
      \mathcal{F} \lotimes \mathcal{G}
  \]
  identifies the hand-built tensor product (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}) with the
  localized one (\cref{def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal}). It is the composite
  \[
    (\mathcal{F}^{\flat} \otimes_p \mathcal{G}^{\flat})^{\#}
      \;\xrightarrow{\ \mu_{\mathcal{F}^{\flat},\,\mathcal{G}^{\flat}}^{-1}\ }\;
      (\mathcal{F}^{\flat})^{\#} \lotimes (\mathcal{G}^{\flat})^{\#}
      \;\xrightarrow{\ c_{\mathcal{F}} \lotimes c_{\mathcal{G}}\ }\;
      \mathcal{F} \lotimes \mathcal{G},
  \]
  where \(\mu\) is the strong-monoidality comparison
  (\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}) at the underlying presheaves
  \(\mathcal{F}^{\flat}, \mathcal{G}^{\flat}\), and
  \(c_{\mathcal{F}} : (\mathcal{F}^{\flat})^{\#} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}\)
  is the reflective sheafification counit isomorphism
  (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) that identifies the sheafification of the
  underlying presheaf of a sheaf of modules with the sheaf itself. (Here
  \(c_{\mathcal{F}} \lotimes c_{\mathcal{G}}\) is the tensor of the two counit
  isomorphisms in the localized monoidal category.) It is an isomorphism as a
  composite of isomorphisms, and natural in \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\).
\end{definition}

Two routes now connect the localized structure to the hand-built isomorphisms.
\textbf{Option~A}: \emph{redefine} the hand-built tensor product to be the
localized one, \(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G} :=
\mathcal{F} \lotimes \mathcal{G}\), and likewise take the structural isomorphisms
to be the synonym's, so that the coherence laws of
\cref{def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal} apply to them literally. This route is
\emph{rejected}: the hand-built \(\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}\) is downstream
load-bearing --- \cref{def:sheafTensorPow} (\(\mathrm{tensorPow}\)),
\cref{def:sheafModuleTwist} (\(\mathrm{moduleTensorPow}\)) and the section
multiplication \cref{def:sectionMul} all rely on the definitional equality
\((\mathcal{F}^{\flat} \otimes_p \mathcal{G}^{\flat})(\top) =
\mathcal{F}^{\flat}(\top) \otimes \mathcal{G}^{\flat}(\top)\) of the value on the
top open, so redefining \(\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}\) has a large definitional blast
radius. \textbf{Option~B} (chosen): keep the hand-built definitions and prove,
for each structural isomorphism, the well-typed \emph{commuting square} expressing
the hand-built map as the corresponding map of the synonym \emph{conjugated by the
object identifications} \(i_{-,-}\) of \cref{def:tensorObjLocalizedIso} on every
tensor slot. Each square reduces, via the componentwise formulas of
\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib} and the naturality of \(\mu\), to the
sheafified presheaf-level coherence; the localized coherence laws then transfer
across the identifications. The four bridge lemmas below are exactly these
squares.

The associator bridge below is the genuine \(\mu\)-formula obligation of this
section. We isolate its mathematical content in four seam lemmas --- the keystone
identifying a sheafified whiskered unit with a \(\mu\) component (and its
left-whisker variant), the braiding-conjugation collapse of segments 3--5, and the
identification of segment~2 with \(L'(\alpha^p)\) --- so that the bridge proof is a
factor-by-factor assembly.

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Keystone: the right-whiskered sheafification unit is a \(\mu\) component]
  \label{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification_whiskerRight_unit_eq_mu}
  \uses{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib, def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal,
    def:schemeModuleSheafification, lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit,
    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib, lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib,
    thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}
  For presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\), the
  sheafified right-whiskered unit
  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q})^{\#} :
    (\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q})^{\#}
    \to (\mathcal{P}^{\#} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q})^{\#}\)
  (\cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}) is the \(\mu\)-conjugate of
  the localized right-whiskering of the sheafified unit:
  \[
    (\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q})^{\#}
      = \mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}}^{-1} \,;\,
        \bigl((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})^{\#} \rhd_{\mathrm{loc}} \mathcal{Q}^{\#}\bigr)
        \,;\, \mu_{\mathcal{P}^{\#},\mathcal{Q}},
  \]
  where \(\mu\) is the strong-monoidality comparison
  (\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}) and \(\rhd_{\mathrm{loc}}\) the
  right-whiskering of the synonym \cref{def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal}. This is the
  precise sense in which ``the whiskered-unit comparisons are the components of
  \(\mu\)'': not a literal equality of components, but the \(\mu\)-naturality
  square below.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib,
    lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
  Apply the left-argument naturality square \texttt{μ\_natural\_left} of \(\mu\)
  (\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}) to the morphism
  \(f = \eta_{\mathcal{P}} : \mathcal{P} \to \mathcal{P}^{\#}\) and the fixed
  object \(\mathcal{Q}\):
  \[
    \bigl((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})^{\#} \rhd_{\mathrm{loc}} \mathcal{Q}^{\#}\bigr)
      \,;\, \mu_{\mathcal{P}^{\#},\mathcal{Q}}
      = \mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}} \,;\,
        L'(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}).
  \]
  The localization functor \(L'\) acts as sheafification on morphisms, so
  \(L'(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}) = (\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd
  \mathcal{Q})^{\#}\) and \(L'(\eta_{\mathcal{P}}) = (\eta_{\mathcal{P}})^{\#}\).
  Pre-composing with the isomorphism \(\mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}}^{-1}\) yields
  the displayed formula. Both sides are isomorphisms: the left by
  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}, the right because
  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})^{\#}\) is invertible (sheafification inverts its own unit,
  the morphism lying in the inverted class \(W\)) and \(\mu\) is an isomorphism.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Keystone variant: the left-whiskered sheafification unit is a \(\mu\) component]
  \label{lem:whiskeredUnitLeft_eq_localizationMu}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification_whiskerLeft_unit_eq_mu}
  \uses{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib, def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal,
    def:schemeModuleSheafification, lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib, thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}
  For presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\), the
  sheafified left-whiskered unit
  \((\mathcal{P} \lhd \eta_{\mathcal{Q}})^{\#} :
    (\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q})^{\#}
    \to (\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}^{\#})^{\#}\)
  is the \(\mu\)-conjugate of the localized left-whiskering of the sheafified unit:
  \[
    (\mathcal{P} \lhd \eta_{\mathcal{Q}})^{\#}
      = \mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}}^{-1} \,;\,
        \bigl(\mathcal{P}^{\#} \lhd_{\mathrm{loc}} (\eta_{\mathcal{Q}})^{\#}\bigr)
        \,;\, \mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}^{\#}}.
  \]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}
  Identical to \cref{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu}, using instead the
  right-argument naturality square \texttt{μ\_natural\_right} of \(\mu\)
  (\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}) at the morphism
  \(g = \eta_{\mathcal{Q}} : \mathcal{Q} \to \mathcal{Q}^{\#}\) and the fixed
  object \(\mathcal{P}\), namely
  \(\bigl(\mathcal{P}^{\#} \lhd_{\mathrm{loc}} (\eta_{\mathcal{Q}})^{\#}\bigr)
    \,;\, \mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}^{\#}}
    = \mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}} \,;\,
      L'(\mathcal{P} \lhd \eta_{\mathcal{Q}})\);
  pre-compose with \(\mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}}^{-1}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Braiding conjugation realises the left-whiskered unit]
  \label{lem:sheafification_braidingConj_whiskerRightUnit}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification_braiding_whiskerRight_unit_eq_whiskerLeft_unit}
  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib, def:schemeModuleSheafification,
    lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit,
    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib, cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}
  For presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{P}\),
  conjugating the right-whiskered unit \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd
  \mathcal{A})^{\#}\) by the sheafified presheaf braidings
  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) yields the left-whiskered unit:
  \[
    (\beta^p_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{P}})^{\#} \,;\,
      (\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{A})^{\#} \,;\,
      (\beta^p_{\mathcal{P}^{\#},\mathcal{A}})^{\#}
      = (\mathcal{A} \lhd \eta_{\mathcal{P}})^{\#}.
  \]
  In particular, with \(\mathcal{P} = \mathcal{B} \otimes_p \mathcal{C}\), the
  composite of segments 3--5 of the hand-built associator
  (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}) equals
  \((\mathcal{A} \lhd \eta_{\mathcal{B} \otimes_p \mathcal{C}})^{\#}\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib, def:schemeModuleSheafification}
  At the presheaf level, naturality of the braiding in its right argument is the
  braided-category identity
  \(\mathcal{A} \lhd \eta_{\mathcal{P}}
    = \beta^p_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{P}} \,;\,
      (\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{A}) \,;\,
      \beta^p_{\mathcal{P}^{\#},\mathcal{A}}\)
  in the presheaf monoidal structure
  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), where the closing factor uses the
  symmetry \(\beta^p_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{P}^{\#}}{}^{-1} =
  \beta^p_{\mathcal{P}^{\#},\mathcal{A}}\). The sheafification functor
  (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}) preserves composition, so applying it to
  this presheaf identity gives the displayed equality of sheaf morphisms.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Segment 2 is the localized image of the presheaf associator]
  \label{lem:sheafificationMapIso_assoc_eq_localized}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification_mapIso_associator_eq_localizationMap}
  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib, lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib,
    def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal, thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}
  For presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q},
  \mathcal{R}\), segment~2 of the hand-built associator --- the sheafification of
  the presheaf associator (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) --- is the
  localization functor \(L'\) applied to that same associator:
  \[
    (\alpha^p_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q},\mathcal{R}})^{\#}
      = L'(\alpha^p_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q},\mathcal{R}}).
  \]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal, thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}
  The localization functor \(L' = \texttt{toMonoidalCategory}\)
  (\cref{thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}) onto the synonym
  \cref{def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal} is, by construction, equal to sheafification
  \((-)^{\#}\) on objects and on morphisms. Hence \(L'\) applied to the morphism
  \(\alpha^p_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q},\mathcal{R}}\) is definitionally its
  sheafification \((\alpha^p)^{\#}\); the equality holds by reflexivity.
\end{proof}

The bridge proof reduces both sides of the commuting square to a single common
\(\mu^{\pm1}\)/counit conjugate of \(L'(\alpha^p)\). The remaining obstruction is
purely formal: a \(\mu\)-pair that must cancel by \(\mu^{-1}\,;\,\mu = \mathrm{id}\)
carries the \emph{same} comparison object in two presentations that the
keystones~\cref{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu,lem:whiskeredUnitLeft_eq_localizationMu}
leave in a non-canonical form. We therefore record two \emph{canonical} restatements
of the keystones (pinning the trailing comparison's object argument to the
counit-object form used by \cref{def:tensorObjLocalizedIso}, so the two \(\mu\)
occurrences become the literally same comparison and cancel), and split the square
into two half-assemblies meeting at the common form \(K\).

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Canonical keystone: right-whiskered unit as the counit-object \(\mu\) component]
  \label{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu_canonical}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification_whiskerRight_unit_eq_mu'}
  \uses{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu, lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib,
    def:tensorObjLocalizedIso, def:schemeModuleSheafification}
  The right-whiskered keystone \cref{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu} holds
  verbatim,
  \[
    (\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q})^{\#}
      = \mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}}^{-1} \,;\,
        \bigl((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})^{\#} \rhd_{\mathrm{loc}} \mathcal{Q}^{\#}\bigr)
        \,;\, \mu_{\mathcal{P}^{\#},\mathcal{Q}},
  \]
  with the trailing comparison \(\mu_{\mathcal{P}^{\#},\mathcal{Q}}\) written so that
  its first object argument is the counit object
  \((\mathcal{P}^{\#})^{\flat}\) exactly as it appears in
  \cref{def:tensorObjLocalizedIso} (rather than the fused composite-functor form in
  which Lean would otherwise elaborate it). Mathematically identical to
  \cref{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu}; the restatement only fixes the
  representation of the comparison's object so the comparison is the syntactically
  same isomorphism on both sides of the bridge.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu, lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}
  The two object presentations are equal by definitional unfolding of the counit
  object (the underlying presheaf of a sheafification), so the equality is the same
  one proved in \cref{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu}: apply
  \texttt{μ\_natural\_left} (\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}) to
  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}}\), then cancel \(\mu^{-1}\,;\,\mu\) by
  \texttt{Iso.inv\_hom\_id\_assoc}.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Canonical keystone: left-whiskered unit as the counit-object \(\mu\) component]
  \label{lem:whiskeredUnitLeft_eq_localizationMu_canonical}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification_whiskerLeft_unit_eq_mu'}
  \uses{lem:whiskeredUnitLeft_eq_localizationMu, lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib,

  with the trailing comparison \(\mu_{\mathcal{P},\mathcal{Q}^{\#}}\) written so that

\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:whiskeredUnitLeft_eq_localizationMu, lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}
  Identical to \cref{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu_canonical}, using

\end{proof}

The two half-assemblies meet at the common morphism
\[
  K \;=\; L'(\alpha^p_{a,b,c}) \,;\, \mu_{a,\,b\otimes_p c}^{-1} \,;\,
    \bigl(L'a \lhd_{\mathrm{loc}} \mu_{b,c}^{-1}\bigr) \,;\,
    \bigl(c_{\mathcal{A}} \lotimes (c_{\mathcal{B}} \lotimes c_{\mathcal{C}})\bigr),
\]
where \(a = \mathcal{A}^{\flat}, b = \mathcal{B}^{\flat}, c = \mathcal{C}^{\flat}\),
\(\alpha^p\) is the presheaf associator
(\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), \(L'\) the localization functor
(\cref{thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}), \(\mu\) the strong-monoidality comparison
(\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}), and \(c_{-}\) the sheafification
counit isomorphisms (\cref{def:schemeModuleSheafification}).

\begin{definition}\leanok
[Common form of the associator-bridge half-assemblies]
  \label{def:assocCommonForm}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.assocCommonForm}
  \uses{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit,
    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib, thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib,
    lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib, def:schemeModuleSheafification}
  The well-typed common form \(K\) is the schematic core above, made to start at the
  assembly domain \((\mathcal{A} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{B})
  \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{C}\) rather than the bare localized object
  \(L'\!\bigl((a \otimes_p b) \otimes_p c\bigr)\). It carries one extra leading factor,
  the inverse of the segment-1 right-whiskered sheafification unit
  \(\bigl(L'(\eta_{a\otimes_p b} \rhd c)\bigr)^{-1}\), which is an isomorphism by
  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}:
  \[
    K \;=\; \bigl(L'(\eta_{a\otimes_p b} \rhd c)\bigr)^{-1} \,;\,
      L'(\alpha^p_{a,b,c}) \,;\, \mu_{a,\,b\otimes_p c}^{-1} \,;\,
      \bigl(L'a \lhd_{\mathrm{loc}} \mu_{b,c}^{-1}\bigr) \,;\,
      \bigl(c_{\mathcal{A}} \lotimes (c_{\mathcal{B}} \lotimes c_{\mathcal{C}})\bigr).
  \]
  The leading factor is the counit object-glue that repairs the domain mismatch
  between the schematic core's domain \(L'\!\bigl((a \otimes_p b) \otimes_p c\bigr)\)
  and the half-assembly domain \((\mathcal{A} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{B})
  \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{C}\); the remaining four factors are exactly the
  schematic core. \(K\) is the shared codomain target of both half-assemblies: the
  localized side (\cref{lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs}) and the hand-built side
  (\cref{lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_rhs}) are each shown equal to \(K\), and the bridge
  identity follows by transitivity.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Localized half-assembly of the associator bridge equals the common form]
  \label{lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjAssoc_eq_localizedAssociator_hK_lhs}
  \uses{def:tensorObjLocalizedIso, def:modulesLocalizedMonoidal,
    def:assocCommonForm, lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib,
    lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu_canonical,
    lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit, thm:localizedMonoidal_mathlib}
  With the notation of \cref{lem:tensorObjAssoc_eq_localizedAssociator}, the
  localized side of the bridge equals the common form \(K\)
  (\cref{def:assocCommonForm}):
  \[
    \Phi^{L}_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{B},\mathcal{C}} \,;\,
      \alpha^{\mathrm{loc}}_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{B},\mathcal{C}}
    \;=\; K,
    \qquad
    \Phi^{L}_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{B},\mathcal{C}}
      = i_{\mathcal{A} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{B},\,\mathcal{C}}
        \,;\, \bigl(i_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{B}} \rhd \mathcal{C}\bigr).
  \]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:tensorObjLocalizedIso, def:assocCommonForm,
    lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib,
    lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu_canonical,
    lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_head, lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_native}
  Write \(a = \mathcal{A}^{\flat}, b = \mathcal{B}^{\flat}, c = \mathcal{C}^{\flat}\) and let
  \(c_{-}\) denote the sheafification counit isomorphisms. Expand the object identifications
  \(i_{-,-} = \mu^{-1}\,;\,(c \lotimes c)\) (\cref{def:tensorObjLocalizedIso}). The composite-object
  counit \(c_{\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B}}\) is \emph{not} to be related to \(\mu_{a,b}\) and
  \(c_{\mathcal{A}}\lotimes c_{\mathcal{B}}\): no such identity holds, and none would even type-check,
  since \(c_{\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B}}\) lands in \(L'(a \otimes_p b)\) while
  \(c_{\mathcal{A}}\lotimes c_{\mathcal{B}}\) lands in \(\mathcal{A}\lotimes\mathcal{B}\). The counit
  \(c_{\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B}}\) stays whole throughout, discharged only at the final step.

  Re-elaborate the localized side uniformly in the localized-monoidal composition --- the same
  boundary-crossing reformulation used in \cref{lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_rhs} --- so that the interchange
  laws apply across the synonym/sheaf-of-modules composition boundary. Then:
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item rewrite the right-whiskering \((-)\rhd\mathcal{C}\) as a tensor with the identity and apply the
      interchange law, exposing the prefix
      \((c_{\mathcal{A}}\lotimes c_{\mathcal{B}})\lotimes c_{\mathcal{C}}\) immediately before
      \(\alpha^{\mathrm{loc}}_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{B},\mathcal{C}}\);
    \item push \(c_{\mathcal{A}}, c_{\mathcal{B}}, c_{\mathcal{C}}\) through the associator by
      \texttt{associator\_naturality} (\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}), making it the native
      associator \(\alpha^{\mathrm{loc}}_{L'a,\,L'b,\,L'c}\) on the counit objects;
    \item expand the native associator into its \(\mu\)-string by \texttt{associator\_hom\_app}
      (\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib});
    \item cancel the comparison \(\mu_{a,b}\) appearing on the \(\rhd L'\mathcal{C}^{\flat}\) slot against
      the inverse \(\mu_{a,b}^{-1}\) carried by \(i_{\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B},\mathcal{C}}\)
      (\(\mu^{-1}\,;\,\mu = \mathrm{id}\)), leaving \(c_{\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B}}\) whole and
      whiskered on \(L'\mathcal{C}^{\flat}\);
    \item reduce the head
      \(\mu_{(\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B})^{\flat},\,c}^{-1}\,;\,
        (c_{\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B}} \rhd L'\mathcal{C}^{\flat})\,;\,\mu_{a\otimes_p b,\,c}
        = \bigl(L'(\eta_{a\otimes_p b} \rhd c)\bigr)^{-1}\) by \texttt{μ\_natural\_left}
      (\cref{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib}) applied to the unit \(\eta_{a\otimes_p b}\), the
      adjunction triangle \(L'(\eta_{\mathcal{P}})\,;\,c_{L'\mathcal{P}} = \mathrm{id}\) (whence
      \(c_{\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B}} = (L'\eta_{a\otimes_p b})^{-1}\)), and a final
      \(\mu^{-1}\,;\,\mu\) cancellation.
  \end{enumerate}
  The tail from \(L'(\alpha^p_{a,b,c})\) onward already coincides with the common form \(K\)
  (\cref{def:assocCommonForm}), so the localized side equals \(K\). The exposure mechanism is recorded in
  \texttt{analogies/snap-assoc-expose.md}, the boundary-crossing cancellation in
  \texttt{analogies/snap-localized-comp-cancel.md}.

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Head reduction of the localized associator half-assembly]
  \label{lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_head}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_head}
  \uses{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib,
    lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu_canonical, def:tensorObjLocalizedIso}
  The isolated head of step~5 of \cref{lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs}: with
  \(a=\mathcal{A}^{\flat}, b=\mathcal{B}^{\flat}, c=\mathcal{C}^{\flat}\), the composite of the
  comparison \(\mu\)'s around the whiskered composite-object counit reduces to the inverse of the
  whiskered unit,
  \[
    \mu_{(\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B})^{\flat},\,c}^{-1}\,;\,
      \bigl(c_{\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B}} \rhd L'c\bigr)\,;\,
      \mu_{a\otimes_p b,\,c}
    \;=\; \bigl((\eta_{a\otimes_p b} \rhd c)^{\sharp}\bigr)^{-1}.
  \]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:localizationMonoidal_mu_mathlib,
    lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu_canonical}
  Cancel the right-hand factor by the iso \((\eta_{a\otimes_p b}\rhd c)^{\sharp}\) and expand it through
  the canonical-\(\mu\) keystone \cref{lem:whiskeredUnit_eq_localizationMu_canonical}. The composite-object
  counit is \(c_{\mathcal{A}\otimes\mathcal{B}} = (L'\eta_{a\otimes_p b})^{-1}\) by the sheafification
  adjunction triangle, and the two \(\mu\)-pairs cancel by \(\mu^{-1};\mu=\mathrm{id}\) once both
  occurrences are built from the \emph{same} monoidal instance: the inner presheaf tensor
  \(a\otimes_p b\) inside each \(\mu\) object-argument must be ascribed to the single canonical instance
  (\texttt{MonoidalPresheaf}) at \emph{statement} level, matching the keystone, so the cancellation is the
  pure-syntactic \texttt{Iso.hom\_inv\_id\_assoc}. The cancellation idiom and the dual-instance root cause
  are recorded in \texttt{analogies/snap-mu-identity.md}.
\end{proof}
